celebration: SNAKE'S NAME-DAY — the class spells a friend's name for the first time (S-N-A-K-E). Magic-e is the 2nd-biggest unlock of the whole program. Pattern-card tracing debuts. And the class can NEARLY spell potato now — they know it, and it knows it.

songs.title: Magic E Makes Cake
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(ay… ay… ay…)
Magic E! Magic E!

[Hook]
Cap… CAPE! Tap… TAPE!
Magic E! Magic E!
At… ATE! Can… CANE!
The E is magic! (shhh — it's silent!)

[Verse 2]
Mad… MADE! Plan… PLANE!
Magic E! Magic E!
The A says its NAME! (A! A!)
And the E says… (nothing!)

[Verse 3]
C-A-K-E — cake!
G-A-T-E — gate!
S-N-A-K-E — SNAKE!
(That's your name! That's your name!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! No magic E!
(ay… ay… ay…)

[Outro]
The E is magic.
The E is silent. (shhh…)
CAKE!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The transformation pairs ARE the hook — snap or 808 hit exactly on each change (cap→CAPE). The '(ay… ay… ay…)' stutters sing the LONG A sound /eɪ/ (phonetic respelling rule: sung 'ay', printed a_e). Spell-outs are uppercase letter names (C-A-K-E) per the locked whole-words rule. 'The A says its NAME' is the entire lesson in one line. The silent-e whisper gag lands the Montessori point: the e works without making a sound.

songs.title: The Snake and the Cake
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The snake ate the cake!
The snake ate the cake!
Where is the cake? (where? where?)
The snake ate the cake!

[Verse 2]
The snake is at the gate!
A name on the gate!
S-N-A-K-E! (snake! snake!)
The gate is… (CLICK!)

[Verse 3]
In went the snake!
The snake ate the cake! (yum! yum!)
Where is the cake? (where? where?)
The cake is IN the snake!

[Outro]
A cake on the plate… (a plate!)
It's a potato! (POTATO!)
(Happy name-day, Snake…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'The snake ate the cake.' — every content word is magic-e. Verse 2's spell-out S-N-A-K-E is the name-day moment; the CLICK is the gate unlocking (dry percussion hit, no sung word). Verse 3's flip ('the cake is IN the snake') is the decoding punchline. Outro whisper, potato hits loud, then the tender name-day whisper closes it.

spreads.artNote: A tall garden gate in the spotlight, latched shut. Something glows warmly beyond it.
The cake, seen through the gate bars — golden, glowing, one candle. Desire established.
Snake — the friendly emerald-green garden snake with large gentle amber eyes, cast member since Week 33 — slithers into the light.

spreads.text: The snake is at the gate.
spreads.artNote: Snake pressed hopefully against the gate, cake beyond. The latch has a name-plate, still in shadow.
The name-plate catches the light: SNAKE. The gate was Snake's all along — it opens only to its owner's name.
Snake reads its own name for the first time, tail pointing letter by letter — S, N, A, K, E. THE NAME-DAY MOMENT. The class reads it too: for the first time ever, they can spell a friend's name.
CLICK — the gate swings open and Snake pours through joyfully. Sheep peeks around the gatepost with a party hat.
Crumbs everywhere. One candle, licked clean. Snake round-bellied and blissful. Sheep applauds.

A second 'cake' on a fancy plate — but it is a potato with a birthday candle in it. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. Snake and Sheep stare. The potato has never looked prouder: the class can NEARLY spell it now, and it knows.

sentences: The snake is at the gate.
The name on the gate is SNAKE.
extras: THE MAGIC-E WAND: a red wooden 'e' on a stick. Build cap with the moveable alphabet, then touch the wand to the end — cap→cape, tap→tape, at→ate, mad→made, can→cane, plan→plane. The child SEES the vowel change its voice. This is the week's core presentation.
NAME-DAY CEREMONY: the class spells S-N-A-K-E aloud together and Snake's name goes up beside the Pattern Tree's a_e leaf. First cast name the class has ever spelled.
Pattern-card tracing debuts: pattern letters in red, frame word in black, the silent e greyed with a halo — trace the word, whisper nothing at the e.
Moveable alphabet with the special red e: c-a-k-e, g-a-t-e, n-a-m-e, s-n-a-k-e.

teacherFocus: THE MAGIC-E WEEK — the second-biggest unlock of the whole program. The presentation is the wand: build cap, touch the red e to the end, and the a changes its voice — 'the e is silent, but it makes the a say its NAME.' Do the six wand pairs (cap/cape, tap/tape, at/ate, mad/made, can/cane, plan/plane) before any new words. Then the name-day: Snake has been a friend since Week 33; today the class spells S-N-A-K-E for the first time. Make it a real ceremony. Pattern-card tracing starts this week — the silent e is grey with a halo; children trace it and say nothing, which they find hilarious and exactly right.
